sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check what changed after pressing UP. I need to compare the previous frame to the current frame to see if the player (green block) moved.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after UP action
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']

# Find green node (player) in both frames
def find_node_by_color(nodes, color):
    for n in nodes:
        if n['color'] == color:
            return n
    return None

green_before = find_node_by_color(before, 'N')
green_after = find_node_by_color(after, 'N')

print(f"Green before: boundary={green_before['boundary'] if green_before else 'None'}")
print(f"Green after: boundary={green_after['boundary'] if green_after else 'None'}")

# Also check white node
white_before = find_node_by_color(before, 'W')
white_after = find_node_by_color(after, 'W')
print(f"White before: boundary={white_before['boundary'] if white_before else 'None'}")
print(f"White after: boundary={white_after['boundary'] if white_after else 'None'}")

# Check pink bar (HUD)
pink_before = find_node_by_color(before, 'P')
pink_after = find_node_by_color(after, 'P')
print(f"Pink before: pixels={pink_before['pixels'] if pink_before else 'None'}")
print(f"Pink after: pixels={pink_after['pixels'] if pink_after else 'None'}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

White after: boundary=[[44, 32], [44, 35]]

Pink after: pixels=64

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The player moved UP by 4 rows:
- Green: from rows 49-51 to rows 45-47 (moved up 4)
- White: from row 48 to row 44 (moved up 4)
So UP moves the player up by 4 rows. The player is now at rows 45-47. Let me continue moving UP to reach the blue targets. The targets are at:
- Node 7/8: rows 36-39, cols 32-35 (directly above)
- Node 3/5: rows 28-31, cols 16-19 (left)
- Node 1/2: rows 24-27, cols 44-47 (right)
The player is currently at cols 32-35, so the target at rows 36-39, cols 32-35 is directly above. Let me move UP more to reach it.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player moved up 4 rows. Target directly above is at rows 36-39, cols 32-35
# Player now at rows 45-47. Need to go up more to reach target at 36-39
# Each UP moves 4 rows, so one more UP should put us at rows 41-43
# Let's try multiple UPs to reach the targets

action(['UP', 'UP', 'UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
